typography.font_body: 'Karla', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
typography.font_mono: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, 'SF Mono', Menlo, Consolas, 'Liberation Mono', monospace

style_notes: Bluebonnet blue (#3E4C76) leads, anchored in the Texas wildflower rather than coastal-cottage or farmhouse, with kraft-paper tan (#B8915E) used sparingly for the stamped badge, dividers, and icon detail to supply the handmade-materials warmth that blue alone lacks. The background is a pale blue-white (#EEF1F5), deliberately not warm cream, and the type pairing is Bitter (a sturdy slab serif in bold/black weights, used only for the wordmark and headings) over Karla for body — chosen to read like a rustic hand-stamp and to avoid the overused high-contrast-serif-on-cream-with-terracotta look the client and research both reject. Note: the brief's display options were 'Yeseva One or Bitter in a semibold/black weight,' but Yeseva One ships a single 400 weight only, so Bitter is the sole option that satisfies the requested heavier weight; the dusty sky blue secondary (#8294B8) is mapped to primary_light for highlights and softer UI. Everything stays quiet and disciplined — moderate radii, low indigo-tinted shadows, and motion limited to a subtle product-card hover that must honor prefers-reduced-motion — so the one circular stamped maker's-badge is the single bold moment. The client-provided palette is internally coherent and meets WCAG AA for body text on background and surface; no concerns to flag.
